description: Small hummingbird (about 4" in length), with a moderately short (about 1 inch), straight, black bill. Green on upperparts turning to gray on front (above bill), with matt-black wings. Upperparts of outer tail feathers (R3-R5) basally gray-green, with uneven subterminal blackish band, and variable white terminus ranging from almost no white (R3) to large white tip (R5). Underparts off-white except for white patch on the lower belly, faint dark-green spotting on sides of belly and breast, and triangular patch of dark scales on the center of the throat. The eye was black, with a dark gray diffuse auricular patch extending back from the eye, and a small black patch of feathers in front of the eye. A thin whitish line bordered the upper side of the eye, and the auricular patch, expanding into a bright white spot just behind the eye.Behaviors: Perched on a thin branch of a bush or a tree near a hummingbird feeder, with frequent prolonged visits to the feeder, where it mostly perched while feeding, and occasionally hovered near the feeder.Call: nonePlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: N Komar, N Komar, Brad Biggerstaff, Brad Biggerstaff, Brad Biggerstaff
